title: Daily Routines
shortSummary: Describe your day from morning to night using common reflexive and daily verbs.
culturalNote: The Brazilian day starts with a light café da manhã (breakfast), often bread, fruit, and coffee. Lunch (o almoço) is traditionally the main meal, while dinner (o jantar) can be lighter. Many daily-routine actions use reflexive verbs in Portuguese: you 'wake yourself' and 'get yourself up'. Brazilians value sociability, so coffee breaks (o cafezinho) and a chat are part of the working rhythm. The verb costumar (to usually do) is handy for describing habits: eu costumo acordar cedo (I usually wake up early).

grammar.explanation: Some routine verbs are reflexive in Portuguese and take a pronoun: levantar-se (to get up), sentir-se (to feel). In Brazil the pronoun comes before the verb: eu me levanto, ele se levanta.
grammar.commonMistake: Forgetting the pronoun, or placing it after the verb in Brazilian usage.

grammar.explanation: To say at what time something happens, use à or às plus the hour: às sete (at seven), ao meio-dia (at midday). Às is for plural hours, à for one o'clock.
grammar.commonMistake: Forgetting the crase accent on às when stating a time.

dialogue.setup: Bruno tells his friend Marina about his weekday.

lines.speaker: Marina
lines.english: What time do you wake up?

lines.speaker: Bruno
lines.native: Eu acordo às seis e meia.
lines.english: I wake up at half past six.

lines.speaker: Bruno
lines.native: Tomo café e vou para o trabalho.
lines.english: I have breakfast and go to work.

lines.speaker: Marina
lines.english: What time do you come home?

lines.speaker: Bruno
lines.native: Por volta das seis, e janto às oito.
lines.english: Around six, and I have dinner at eight.

quiz.question: How do you say 'I wake up at seven' in Portuguese?
quiz.explanation: Acordar means to wake up; às sete is 'at seven'.

quiz.question: Why does 'eu me levanto' include 'me'?
quiz.explanation: Reflexive verbs need a matching pronoun like me.

quiz.question: Trabalho _____ nove. (at, plural hours)
quiz.explanation: Às is used before plural hours.

quiz.question: What does tomo café da manhã mean?
quiz.explanation: Tomar café da manhã means to have breakfast.

quiz.question: Arrange: dormir / vou
quiz.explanation: Vou dormir means 'I go to sleep'.
